comments.author: @PietMamabolo-o8i
comments.text: I ll visit and carry a wife and see whether I can carry her

comments.author: @juliankmiec5081
comments.text: My sauna has a line in it, "Resist the urge to go from hot sauna to shower. Your pores are wide open, anything in that water will soon be in you". As a sufferer with ALS which I believe is caused by a gut bacteria. I would propose rethinking that run from sauna and jump into pond.

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: What the world calls a luxury spa, the Finns call a basic human right: the Sauna! It’s hilarious how they love their extreme personal space in public, but will happily sit completely naked with strangers in a 180°F room. This documentary perfectly captured the introverted charm and the beautiful, untouched landscape of Finland in 2026

comments.author: @JohnP-xh2yr
comments.text: It’s pronounced Sow Na😡. Not Saw Na 😉🇫🇮🙏🏼❤️ and not pula, pronounced bula😊

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Thank you so much for the kind correction, my Finnish friend. I truly appreciate it. Pronunciation matters, especially when we are talking about someone’s culture and language. I’ll keep “Sow-na” and “bula” in mind for the future. Thanks for helping us learn with respect🙏❤️

comments.author: @EuropeExploresAmerica
comments.text: I'm still trying to wrap my head around the sauna-to-frozen-lake transition! Does the shock of the cold actually feel refreshing, or does it take years of practice to enjoy?

comments.author: @TrueFactsZA
comments.text: 07:40 So many women, these Finn women are really finishing men on the street.

comments.author: @saggie27
comments.text: I am married so I am not watching it. I am obeying your thumbnail.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: That is very loyal of you, but the video covers plenty of other interesting topics about Finnish life as well!

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: What stands out about Finland isn't just the beauty of its landscapes, but the calm way people value nature, balance, and everyday life. A fascinating lesson in what true happiness can look like.

comments.author: @richardduplessis1090
comments.text: Import 1m Muslims and take the poll again in a couple of years.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: I understand your concern, but Finland’s society is shaped by many things: trust, education, social support, nature, and the way people respect personal space. Every country changes over time, and the real question is how people can live together while protecting peace, safety, and fairness.

comments.author: @courtleyhandy9038
comments.text: I would definitely want to play football ⚽️ in the swamp

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: I would love that too haha. And I love football ⚽️ ⚽️ ⚽️

replies.author: @EuropeExploresAmerica
replies.text: Same here. It looks way more exhausting than regular football though. I can not believe they actually have a world championship for swamp football.

comments.author: @CountryTravelDocumentary
comments.text: Finland feels like a hidden paradise on Earth! 🇫🇮✨ From the magical forests and thousands of lakes to the unique culture and peaceful lifestyle, this country is truly fascinating. This documentary beautifully shows the real beauty of Finland. Amazing work! 👏🌍

comments.author: @LifeNest-3
comments.text: I'd definitely go for swamp football! 🤣 The idea of "Sisu wearing a clown's nose" perfectly explains why Finns are so resilient but still know how to have fun during those long, dark winters.

comments.author: @saivenkatcherukuri
comments.text: Sauna in Sweden was amazing too

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: That sounds wonderful! Sweden definitely has an amazing sauna culture too, and the Nordic way of enjoying heat, silence, and nature feels very special. Finland and Sweden both have their own beautiful traditions. Which sauna experience did you enjoy most there?

comments.author: @RealLifeGlobalDocumentary
comments.text:
Finland is one of the least densely populated countries in Europe, yet it consistently ranks among the highest in trust, education, and quality of life.
Sometimes having more space between people seems to create stronger respect between them

comments.author: @TroyKelley-d9p
comments.text: The happiest place on earth, as long as you're not the wife whose neck was nearly broken when her husband dropped her onto her head while trying to limbo under a log.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Haha, yes, that part definitely makes “the happiest country” sound a little more dangerous than expected. Some of Finland’s quirky competitions are meant to be funny and traditional, but safety should always come first. Thanks for pointing that out in such a sharp way — which Finnish custom in the video surprised you the most?
